reviews.review_msg: Comments here are really dramatic. In reality Silverman is actually a really good prof and easy to talk to. He gives easy exams because he wants his students to actually feel like theyve learned something. The grading scheme makes it so that the avg is B- just like every other bisc/mbb class. He doesnt deserve this crazy low rating imo.
He is an overall great person. Sure his scale is a little tough, but it is very realistic. The course is easy so for you to stand out it is important to outdo the class.

His lectures aren't very helpful, but he will give you a ton of extra information on the topic that you won't be tested on in class. Grading scheme is bad. Honestly, just do yourself a favor, and take it with a different professor because it will be a lot easier.

Alright, many people find this prof hard because of his grading scheme. Yes, an 87 can sometimes be a B+ in his class but its only cause his class average is around 80%. Its not that hard of a course, its memorization mainly (but if you're in bio what did you expect).
ridiculous grading scheme.

The WORST PROF of SFU! I got 69% overall after all my finals and midterms and he gave me a C-. He has his own grading scheme with +/- S.D. which is completely non sense. AVOID HIM! Shouting at students is his nature and he loves doing that. If you are getting a call from your work or anything urgent dont pick it up becoz he will SLAM you...........
Just terrible professor who does not care about student's future. AVOID

This class was ridiculous. His grading scale is absolutely absurd and makes students who actually do well receive low final grades because he decides to SCALE UP a class that doesn't need to be scaled! Very unapproachable in lecture and outside lecture. He yells at students if he sees their phones instead of actually TEACHING. AVOID HIM.
RUINED MY FUTURE! JUST AWFUL GUY THAT YOU SHOULD AVOID AT ANY COST.. WILL BE MY LOWEST MARK IN MY UNI LIFE CAUSE OF HIS BS GRADING SCHEME .. 70% is a C-
AVOID!! only if youre straight A student and able to get 96%.. since A range start from 96

He is okay in the lecture but is fairly unpproachable in his office hour. During the lecture, he usually talked broad rather than focusing one topic. Better to have a good TA who grade the exams and tutorials.

Great lecturer and you will learn tons of cell biology if you come to class and work on the questions he gives before exams; easy A. He is also a very personable and kind person. Very approachable and is a joy to have a conversation with in office hours.
Put in the work required and you'll get an A. Lots of readings

Professor Silverman makes an unpleasant course a great one. His tutorials are designed to make you think, and his midterms are fair. Always open to questions, very knowledgeable and approachable. His interest in the course is evident, and he is known to crack a joke or two during lecture

Yes, he has a crazy grading scale. BUT, if you put in the effort, you'll get the grade. His exams are fair and leniently marked. His notes can be a bit confusing at times, but ask him about it and he clears it up! If you need an amazing grade in this class, it might be a bit of a gamble. However, make sure to take his bisc403!!

Exams were very straight forward. However the conversion to a letter grade was questionable. Low 90's got me an A, but I know several people who got a B+ with very high 80's... no A- as far as I know. Still, I enjoyed the class and found the material very interesting.
I GOT 87% IN THIS COURSE AND GOT A B+. grading scheme is ridiculous and no scale. If you are not going get 90%+ you should avoid .

Exams arent hard, but his grading scheme is definitely bunk! he knows that himself too so he never gives you the exact cut offs for each grade.very arrogant.stops lecture and yells at students when he sees them texting. do urself a favor and stay away from this guy! I don't care if this class is a pre-req! grad a year l8r but don't take his class!!

very bad prof. yes the exams are relatively easy but that fact does not make the prof "good". He doesnt know much about mbb but i dont blame him because hes in bio. his grading scheme COMPLETELY DOESNT MAKE SENCE and he is not able to communicate AT ALL. 88% is a B+ UNREAL

Garbage prof. Avoid at all costs. Exams are easy but grading scheme is completely ridiculous.He uses his useless American grading scheme which doesn't make sence (were in CANADA). Everything he says in class is useless and he does not know anything about the subject himself. I honestly do not know how this guy is allowed to teach

questions are given a week prior to exam(total 4 , none cumulative) and found them not hard to score on.quizzes are based on lectures while exams entirely on article readings. Recommend you make a study partner for preparing/checking your answers for exam. The only challenge were the presentations but by the last one i felt like a beast inside.
Very tough grading scale, need at least a 90 just to score within the A range, above 80 for the B range. He's a decent prof though; study the big picture concepts rather than specific details.

Dr. Silverman is an interesting prof but his marking scheme is ridiculous. To pass with a C- you need to have 70%. His exams are straightforward but he does try to trick you because he makes the exam conceptual rather than straight memorization. Do I recommend this class? Yes and No. If you can put in the effort necessary, then you will do well.
Excellent professor. One of my most enjoyable profs to date. Exams are fair and straightforward. Watch out for the marking scheme though!

as i take his course. i figure he is great. the stuffs he teaches you could actually remember. i meant his style. i think this would be benefit for the students. But to get a great grade could be a challenge. We see how it goes
he is so chill. he told us not to memerize amino acid for the exam. but end up giving us an amino sequence to figure things out. midterm was hard but if your in biology major then u shouuld be fine.

I had Dr. Silverman for MBB 221 as well as BISC 403. I was worried for 403 based on MBB and ratings on here, but he was completely different. You need to listen and take notes in class, but if you do you will do great on exams. You get to prepare answers which I found made the exams rather easy - I definitely recommend this class! :)

I had Dr. Silverman for both MBB 231 and BISC 403. While I found him to be hard in 231, 403 was a different story. In some ways, I think he prepares students for Upper Division courses which is why many people consider him to be hard. You can tell that he's truly dedicated in his work and really tries to help students learn. Recommended.

Despite all the negative reviews, I find Dr.Silverman to be an excellent prof. You can see from the way he lectures that he truly loves what he does and what he teaches. He is an extremely serious, professional and incredibly dedicated person who wants his students to learn. Never had a prof like this and I gotta say I'm really enjoying his class.

Don't take this class unless you like trolling around science databases reading about trafficking. Very grad student oriented. Prof speaks in great English but doesn't use microphone and so difficult to hear him. Expects too much from students. Grading system is too tough. Wouldn't take him again.

This prof is hard, he said it himself on the first day of classes: he expects 403 to be like a med school course..and his expectations are pretty high. Straight scales means 80 is a B, so kind of tough, gives the class the questions for the exam before hand but no indication of how to approach it. Tutorials way too much work.

Agree with what the person below said. I ended with 78% and that was a C+. His tests aren't very efficient in demonstrating the good students from the less good ones. He has a lot of interest in the subject, I'll give him that, but I am DEFINITELY not taking another class with him.

I dont know what planet this guy lives on but lets just say 80% is a C+. End of story. No scaling! THAT'S IT! Besides the unfair marking scheme, he over-complicates the already difficult topics, and dont even bother approaching him to ask questions. Extremely rude. I have to say he is the WORST prof/teacher Ive ever had in my 15 years of education.

Would not recommend this prof, just because of his marking style and his terrible notes. His notes consist of pictures of cells and processes with absolutely no textual information. You will have to depend on your textbook a lot. He marks on the American scale so you wont get an A unless you get 90 and above. 70-80 = C lol. Smart guy though...

Will not tolerate talking in class, cellphones or using laptops for things other than school. That being said, he is very straight forward with his explanations providing you attend lectures and pay attention. Exams were tough but fair. No scaling of grades, so what you get is what you'll end up with. Enjoyable, informative teacher overall.

Very nice and intelligent prof who is clear in his lectures. Lecture notes are usually pictures, which is why you need to listen to what he says and take notes. Lets you know what will be on the exams (Questions are similar to tutorials). Highly recommended!

Great prof. Fair exams. Lectures were super easy to pay attention in because he's a great communicator. He has a large vocabulary which makes it rather comical, and he really knows what he's talking about. This class is not hard, especially if you read the textbook, and especially if you don't try to cram it all the night before.

Good prof overall..he's smart and knows his stuff well. Yes he uses long complicated words sometimes, and yes the notes are filled with lots of pictures. Find the pictures in the textbook and read the captions underneath..i found that very helpful!

His willingness to teach is represented on behalf of his decision of staying home to celebrate AMERICAN thanksgiving rather than coming to school teaching an important topic that is HEAVILY emphasized on the final exam.  This professor is just not very interested into teaching.

Had mbb222,403 etc. Very intelligent professor.He knows his stuff well and is always willing to help.Got my buddy into med school because of his reference letters. He uses big words because that's just how he talks.Great prof, be willing to learn and ask questions. Think conceptually,listen to his lectures and take notes. Best class ever and easy.

Very full of himself and arrogant.  Likes to use big words to make himself look smart.  In some cases certain statements he made in lecture turned out to be dead wrong.  His conduct toward students is often rude and unprofessional. Stay away.

HE is the worst prof at SFU far by the most.  He simply does not know how to teach- I wonder where he got his phD. I do really think that he is full of himself.  his lectures are so boring. it will make you fall asleep. his tests are awful. his markng scheme is terrible. i wonder how he is still a prof.

This man is the worst teacher i have had during my four years at sfu!he also gave me the worst grade i have ever had in my 22 years of life. His notes are not notes, they are pictures from the text book WITHOUT words. once he gave a slide with a pic of processes that occur in the cell..his NOTE for this slide was: 'cell'. the textbook sucks.

HE is so Full of himself, its actually amazing to see it! Marking distribution awful a 87% is a B+! The course has two TAs that mark completely different so you never know what to write down b/c you don't know which TA will mark it. Tutorials are a waste of time and should be better spent reviewing the class material then stupid projects!

He is too high on himself. Very arrogant, thinks he is the smartest dude around. The TA did not like me and I ended up with a lower grade. He does not choose the TAs wisely. Do not take this guy for any course.

if you want to fall asleep in class, be sure to take this class, lectures are very pointless and boring, has pictures on slides with no explanation, only good thing about the class is that he gives you the questions for the exam to take home.

His lectures were good overall. If he confuses us with one part he tries to make it clear next time.  His attitude towards students were very bad... He favors those who are working in a lab -_- gives us exam Q before but make sure to write a lot of details.
Dr. Silverman or the Dr. House the second :). The course is not easy, but he is not a good instructor either. I passed, but I studied a lot. Avoid him if it is possible.

his lectures are dry and he speaks in a monotone voice. you have to go to class to understand his notes as they are usually just figures with minimal writing. average was really low. exams were on the harder side. the M/C was tricky, especially the conceptual questions. the grading scale is different as 60% and below is considered a fail.

his lectures are boring, he speaks in terrible monotone, his notes are useless. the average mark in his class is lower D, just above fail, and there were only 4 As among more than 200 people. anyway, if you feel like memorizing half of the textbook for your final exam, go ahead and take him, and let the force be with you.
Knows his stuff but sometimes hard to pay attention to because he's so monotone and unexpressive.  Tests are difficult but do-able.
I really enjoyed going to his lectures.He's excellent!
Good guy, interesting lectures, articulate speaker. Recommended upper-level elective.

some ppl complained about him, i can understand why, but although i got an okay mark in the class, he's not a bad prof. he talks really slow.. and kinda takes a long time explaining something, but he wants us to understand. midterms and final were on the hard side.. and marking on it was pretty hard..
No Comments
No Comments

I didn't enjoy this class at all-- lectures were boring and vague. He got behind in the notes every class and had to compensate by cramming 3 lectures into one condensed lecture which makes for some fun self-teaching later. Also, he said he would scale the course, but my final mark was an A with 93.5% so I don't think he did.

I really disliked the way this professor taught his lectures. He likes to use boldy phrases like: biosynthetic pathway even though we have no clue what he's talking about. He also didn't give many tips on examinations, so you better know your stuff. I did not like his grade scale. Avoid this professor if possible.

This prof was pretty good, if a little arrogant. One glaring fault though- he could not give you a concise, clear answer to a question. Also, if your just got off the boat, you may have trouble understanding his high-level vocab. Exams were definitely fair, and though time was never abundant, if you knew your stuff you'd have plenty of time.
doesn't care if you do well, as long as he likes his own grading system

Exam questions are vague, but ask for specific things,,, how the hell we know what he's asking for? There will only be an A if you have a 90%. Therefore you must have a cumulative mark of 70% and above to pass this course with him.

His grading scale sux.  He talks a lot but he doesnt know how to explain in a way that make ppl understand.   You get a "D" if you get below 70%.  And his exams test you how fast you can write, not how well you understand the material.  I never have time to double check my answers.
absurd grading scale.
This prof can not teach ... he tries but just can't explain anything clearly in one straight forward sentence ... ahh the lecture seems very boring!

Silverman's got a Rennaissance kinda thing going on, but he comes across genuine, well-spoken and very intelligent.  If you listen to everything he says, do the readings, and participate in discussions, you can easily get 90%+.  He basically gives you exam questions beforehand, and you pick which ones you want to answer.  Highly recommended!

Straightforward notes and good teaching style.  Exams not tricky, just need to understand notes and journal articles.  Since topic, cell biology, is uncertain and developing on present basis, no text book, but informtion is from journal articles you need to read.
Fair exams since they're take home.Very intellectual guy.He really makes you listen in class.Love his old skool hair!!
